title: Change Company Name Service in Kerala- BookMyTM - Trademark & ISO Services in KeralaHow to Change Name of the Company?
h1: How to Change Name of the Company?

blocks.text: How to Change Name of the Company?

Name of a company can be changed at any time after its incorporation with the interest of directors and shareholders. Being a separate legal entity regulated by law, it has to follow a specific process provided by the Companies Act, 2013. Consent from members has to be obtained by conducting a board meeting and once gets consent from members, name reservation and approval from Central Government should be obtained. Fresh Certificate of Incorporation will be issued by RoC in new name after updating the Register of Companies. The change of name does not affect the existence of the company, which leaves all the assets and liabilities unaffected.
Fill the details below for a callback.

Due to any justified reason, the directors and the shareholders mutually may voluntarily decide to change the business name. Name of the company can be used to reflect the vision and characteristic of the business. The existing name requires being change in certain situations like trademark claim etc.Show Less

If the existing name does not reflect the new business activity of the company, name of the company may needed to be changed. If the registrar has directed for change of name to suite business activity, the company must obey the same and apply for change of name.Show Less

Name plays an important role in the value of brand, if it finds the existing name gathering goodwill up to expectation of the owners of the company, they will forced to find a new name. Also with the change or expansion of markets (inside the country or international) it may require considering for a name change to sustain in competitive market conditions.Show Less

Companies doing business regionally mostly uses the names in regional languages or to suite regional culture and while planning for an expansion of market like nationwide or even to international market, the existing name won’t be easily recognizable for the new markets. In certain circumstances, the owners of the company may need to consider for change of name of the company to make it more recognizable in the expanded markets.Show Less
Documents Required to Change Company Name
Certificate of Incorporation of the Company
Digital Signature Certificate of one of authorized director
Copy of latest amended MoA and AoA

Identification plays an important role in making a business outstand in competitive market, build a unique and distinctive name for your business hence enhance your brand’s trust and value. Consumers should identify activity of your business from the brand name so that they can relate your business with its offerings.Even though it meets all the above criteria, once again make sure that the brand name to be short, easy to remember and simple to pronounce or spell.

items: Private Limited Company – Name of the company must end with “Private Limited” (Pvt.Ltd) as a suffix
Public Limited Company – Name must end with “Limited” (Ltd) as a suffix
OPC – The name must end with (OPC) Private Limited as suffix.

items.q: 1. What are the approvals needed for changing the name of the company?
items.a: Answer:To change the company’s name, you must obtain:Consent from the Board of Directors,Consent from the members/shareholders via a resolution,Approval from the concerned Registrar of Companies (RoC), andApproval from the Central Government (via MCA) sc.pages03.net+4Accendoz+4incorpuk.com+4.

items.q: 2. Is a limited company restricted from changing its name?
items.a: Answer:No. A private limited company may freely change its name, subject to compliance with naming rules and regulations. While public companies face more stringent restrictions, private companies must ensure the new name conforms to legal norms Accendoz.

items.q: 3. When should the application for change be made?
items.a: Answer:You must make the application within 30 days of obtaining members’ consent (passed after name reservation).The resolution is notified via Form MGT‑14, andThe name-change application is submitted using Form INC‑24 Accendoz+1Accendoz+1.

items.q: 4. Do MoA & AoA need to be altered?
items.a: Answer:Yes. Both the Memorandum of Association (MoA) and Articles of Association (AoA) must be amended to reflect the new name, and the alteration is filed through Form INC‑24 Accendoz+1Accendoz+1.

items.q: 5. When will the new name of the company be effective?
items.a: Answer:The new name becomes official only after the RoC issues a fresh Certificate of Incorporation bearing the new name Accendoz.

items.q: 6. When should the application for PAN change be made?
items.a: Answer:You must apply to update the company’s PAN and other registrations after receiving the fresh Certificate of Incorporation under the new name .

items.q: 7. What will be the effect of changing the company name?
items.a: Answer:All existing rights and liabilities of the company remain intact.Ongoing legal proceedings continue seamlessly.The company can enforce contracts in its new name.For the next two years, the former name must still be displayed on letterheads, invoices, and official communications
